Use the context7 MCP if you are ever even slightly unsure that you do not have the latest up to date documentation.
Make sure to write unit tests for fine grained debugging details
When it comes to shell commands ESPECIALLY for node, use the tilde ~ instead of the $HOME environment variable.

## Project Context (auto-generated)

For deep codebase understanding, read these files in `.planning/context/`:
- `CODEBASE.md` — File layout, tech stack, dependencies, entry points
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — System layers, state hierarchy, agent pipeline, merge/review flows
- `CONVENTIONS.md` — Naming, module structure, commit format, testing patterns
- `STYLE_GUIDE.md` — Code style, formatting, output conventions, git rules
